title: Helium Supply Chain & Pacific Rim Semiconductor Infrastructure

nodes.label: Pressure Swing Adsorption
nodes.description: Modular gas separation technology enabling small-scale helium extraction at wellheads. Viable at concentrations as low as 0.3%.

nodes.label: Modular Extraction Nodes
nodes.description: Small-scale PSA units ($50K-$250K) deployable at individual wellheads in months, producing crude helium at 50-70% purity.

nodes.label: Virtual Helium Plant
nodes.description: Distributed production model aggregating dozens of small nodes into a coordinated supply chain, inspired by Virtual Power Plants.

nodes.label: Hub-and-Spoke Aggregation
nodes.description: Central purification/liquefaction hub receiving crude helium from distributed nodes. Shared capital, shared risk.

nodes.label: Digital Orchestration Layer
nodes.description: Cloud-based coordination of distributed nodes: inventory tracking, quality assurance, logistics optimization, automated settlement.

nodes.label: I-5 Corridor
nodes.description: PNW Interstate 5 from Bellingham to Medford — cheap electricity, port access, existing distribution, fab proximity.

nodes.label: Silicon Forest
nodes.description: Portland/Hillsboro semiconductor cluster anchored by Intel D1X. 35-40K jobs, $52B+ Intel investment, 50 years of ecosystem development.

nodes.label: Intel D1X Complex
nodes.description: Intel's most advanced development fab in Hillsboro, OR. Leading-edge process development. Largest PNW helium consumer.

nodes.label: Helium in Semiconductor Fabs
nodes.description: Wafer cooling, leak detection, carrier gas, EUV purging. Thousands of liters per month at Grade-A or Research Grade purity.

nodes.label: EUV Lithography
nodes.description: Extreme ultraviolet lithography requires helium-purged environments to prevent light absorption. Critical for leading-edge nodes.

nodes.label: Grade-A Helium (99.999%)
nodes.description: Industrial standard purity for semiconductor and medical applications. Requires centralized purification hub with cryogenic capability.

nodes.label: Research Grade 6.0 (99.9999%)
nodes.description: Highest purity grade for R&D fabs and precision research. Premium pricing, 3-10x above Grade-A.

nodes.label: Closed-Loop Helium Recycling
nodes.description: On-site cryocooler systems capturing 90-95% of helium boil-off. $1.5-3.5M systems with <12 month ROI at crisis prices.

nodes.label: Qatar Force Majeure (March 2026)
nodes.description: March 2, 2026: Qatar Energy halts helium exports. ~33% of global supply offline. Strait of Hormuz closure.

nodes.label: Recurring Shortage Pattern
nodes.description: Five crises since 2006, each worse, intervals shrinking. Root cause: concentrated production, depleting reserves.

nodes.label: Federal Helium Reserve (1925-2021)
nodes.description: Bush Dome, Amarillo TX. ~32 Bcf stockpile sold off under 1996 Privatization Act. Depleted by 2021. Buffer gone.

nodes.label: Helium-3
nodes.description: Rare isotope from tritium decay. ~40-60K L/year global supply. $2-3.5K/L. Critical for quantum computing dilution refrigerators.

nodes.label: Quantum Computing Constraint
nodes.description: Every superconducting qubit processor needs a dilution refrigerator (20-40L He-3/He-4 mix). He-3 supply is a scaling bottleneck.

nodes.label: Microsoft Quantum (Redmond)
nodes.description: Topological qubit research using He-3 dilution refrigerators. PNW anchor for quantum computing He-3 demand.

nodes.label: Interlune (Seattle)
nodes.description: Lunar He-3 extraction company. Founded by ex-Blue Origin president. $18M Series A. Headquartered in Seattle.

nodes.label: PNNL (Richland, WA)
nodes.description: Pacific Northwest National Laboratory. Cryogenic R&D, neutron detection, He-3 alternative development.

nodes.label: Ring of Fire Trading Network
nodes.description: Pacific Rim as interconnected trading system. Mutual dependence: Australia extracts, US refines, Taiwan fabricates, everyone consumes.

nodes.label: East Asian Semiconductor Hubs
nodes.description: TSMC (Kaohsiung), Samsung/SK Hynix (Busan), various (Tokyo). 15-25 day shipping from PNW.

nodes.label: Australia (Amadeus Basin)
nodes.description: Potential 44 Bcf helium resource (~7x annual global demand). 8-14 day shipping to East Asia. Fastest Qatar alternative.

nodes.label: Port of Tacoma / NWSA
nodes.description: West Coast export gateway. 15-25 day container shipping to East Asian semiconductor hubs.

nodes.label: PNW Electricity Advantage
nodes.description: $0.06-$0.08/kWh industrial rate. Significant cost advantage for energy-intensive helium liquefaction.

nodes.label: Crisis Window Economics
nodes.description: Modular PSA IRR >50-100% at crisis prices. Recycling ROI <12 months. CHIPS Act + state grants available. Window opened March 2, 2026.

nodes.label: CHIPS and Science Act
nodes.description: $33B+ grants, $7B loans. Explicitly includes gas supply chain. Intel Oregon awarded up to $8.5B.

nodes.label: Regulatory Landscape
nodes.description: BLM extraction permits, DOT hazmat shipping, WA co-op formation (RCW 23.86), SEPA environmental review. Well-established, navigable.

nodes.label: Co-operative Model
nodes.description: RCW 23.86: five persons, one-member-one-vote. Shared infrastructure, distributed risk, local benefit at every node.
